name: communication-grammar-coach
description: "Use when prompts ask to go over, rewrite, polish, tighten, simplify, or improve docs/README/architecture text, PR notes, or presentation copy for clarity, grammar, tone, and audience fit without losing technical accuracy. Trigger phrases include: make this sound more professional, shorten this writeup, and make this recruiter-friendly."
argument-hint: "Provide the text and target audience (engineer, recruiter, stakeholder, mixed)."
---

# Communication Grammar Coach

## When To Use

- Improve README, docs, PR descriptions, or architecture writeups.
- Rewrite technical content for mixed audiences.
- Tighten grammar without losing technical precision.

## Prompt Examples

- "Go over this README and make it sound more professional."
- "Shorten this architecture writeup for a recruiter audience."
- "Polish these PR notes for clarity without changing technical meaning."

## Procedure

1. Identify target audience and objective.
2. Preserve technical correctness while simplifying wording.
3. Improve structure for scanability (headings, short paragraphs, lists).
4. Remove ambiguity, filler, and repeated phrasing.
5. Return a revised version plus notable changes.

## Guardrails

- Do not alter technical meaning for style alone.
- Keep terminology consistent with project docs and diagrams.
- Prefer concise edits over full rewrites unless requested.
